info.name: Surface
info.comment: Manages display regions for two-dimensional rendered graphics.

description.p: The Surface class represents a rectangular region in the display hierarchy. It manages positioning, visibility, redraws, backing storage and focus for layered user interfaces. Surfaces work with Bitmap objects for rendered pixel data and with the Pointer class for pointer interaction.

A top-level surface is normally backed by an application window on hosted platforms such as Windows and Linux. On native displays, full screen displays and Android, a top-level surface can cover the display without a separate host window. Child surfaces are attached to a parent surface, creating a hierarchy whose ordering, clipping, redraw and input behaviour is managed by this class.

Surface-only interfaces are supported, but most applications should use surfaces as hosts for VectorScene objects. This keeps interfaces resolution independent and matches the rendering path that Kōtuku optimises most heavily.

Surface content is preserved with a backing store where required. Surfaces that share a backing store are composited through their parent, while surfaces that need independent redraw, opacity, video memory or cursor behaviour can own a separate bitmap buffer.

action.name: Activate
action.comment: Shows a surface object on the display.

description.p: For top-level surfaces, Activate() delegates to Show so that the hosted display window becomes visible. Child surfaces are not affected by this action.
Disabled surfaces remain visible but cannot accept user focus through Focus.

action.name: Draw
action.comment: Redraws the contents of a surface object.
action.prototype: ERR acDraw(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height)

input.param: The X position of the region to be drawn.
The Y position of the region to be drawn.
The width of the region to be drawn.
The height of the region to be drawn.

description.p: Calling the Draw action on a surface object will send redraw messages to every hook that has been attached to the surface object's drawing system. This has the effect of redrawing all graphics within the surface object. The procedure is as follows:

list.li: If the surface object's Colour field has been set, the target bitmap will be cleared to that colour.
If the surface is volatile, graphics from background surfaces will be copied to the target bitmap.
Subscribers to the surface object are now called via their hooks so that they can draw to the bitmap.
The bitmap is copied to the video display buffer to complete the process.

description.p: Please be aware that:

list.li: If the target surface contains child surfaces, they will not be redrawn unless they are volatile (using special effects such as transparency, or using the region flag will make a surface volatile).

If the surface object has not had its background colour set, or if the object is not volatile, the bitmap contents will not be automatically cleared (this is advantageous in situations where a particular object will clear the surface area first).

description.p: Enable() clears the disabled state so that the surface can receive focus and normal interaction again.

action.name: Focus
action.comment: Changes the primary user focus to the surface object.

description.p: Focus() makes the surface the primary focus target and notifies the affected focus subscribers. Focus is propagated through the surface hierarchy so that parent surfaces also record inherited focus.

The request is ignored if the surface is disabled, marked with RNF::NO_FOCUS, or outside the active modal surface. If RNF::IGNORE_FOCUS is set, the focus request is forwarded to the parent surface instead.

action.name: Hide
action.comment: Hides a surface object from the display.

description.p: Hide() clears the visible state. For top-level surfaces it hides the hosted display window; for child surfaces it invalidates and exposes the covered parent area so that the background is redrawn. Hiding a modal surface also restores the previous modal surface, or clears modal mode if there is no previous surface.

action.name: LostFocus
action.comment: Informs a surface object that it has lost the user focus.

description.p: LostFocus() clears the RNF::HAS_FOCUS flag when the surface currently holds focus. If the surface has already lost focus, the action returns without further notification.

action.name: Move
action.comment: Moves a surface object to a new display position.
action.prototype: ERR acMove(*Object, DOUBLE DeltaX, DOUBLE DeltaY, DOUBLE DeltaZ)

input.param: The number of units to move along the X axis.
The number of units to move along the Y axis.
The number of units to move along the Z axis.

description.p: Move() applies relative X and Y deltas to the surface. The request honours movement restrictions such as RNF::STICKY, RNF::NO_HORIZONTAL, RNF::NO_VERTICAL and the configured movement limits. Child surfaces are clamped within their parent surface when limits are active.
Queued Move() requests for the same surface may be combined before drawing occurs. After a successful move, redimension subscribers are notified with the surface's updated position and size.

action.name: MoveToBack
action.comment: Moves a surface object to the back of its container.

description.p: For child surfaces, MoveToBack() reorders the surface within its parent while preserving child hierarchy, bitmap ownership constraints, pop-over relationships and RNF::STICK_TO_BACK ordering. Top-level surfaces delegate the request to their hosted display window.

action.name: MoveToFront
action.comment: Moves a surface object to the front of its container.

description.p: For child surfaces, MoveToFront() raises the surface within its parent while preserving child hierarchy, cursor ordering, bitmap ownership constraints, pop-over relationships and RNF::STICK_TO_FRONT ordering. Top-level surfaces delegate the request to their hosted display window.

action.name: MoveToPoint
action.comment: Moves a surface object to an absolute coordinate.
action.prototype: ERR acMoveToPoint(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Z, MTF Flags)

input.param: The new X position to move the object to.
The new Y position to move the object to.
The new Z position to move the object to.
Set the relevant MTF flag for each provided parameter.

description.p: MoveToPoint() converts the supplied absolute X and Y values into relative movement and forwards the request to Move. Coordinates are changed only for axes enabled by the MTF::X and MTF::Y flags.

action.name: Redimension
action.comment: Moves and resizes a surface object in a single action call.
action.prototype: ERR acRedimension(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Z, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height, DOUBLE Depth)
action.tiriPrototype: err = Object.acRedimension(X:num, Y:num, Z:num, Width:num, Height:num, Depth:num)

input.param: The new X position to apply to the target object.
The new Y position to apply to the target object.
The new Z position to apply to the target object.
The new width of the target object.
The new height of the target object.
The new depth of the target object.

action.name: Resize
action.comment: Alters the dimensions of a surface object.
action.prototype: ERR acResize(*Object, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height, DOUBLE Depth)

input.param: The new width of the object.
The new height of the object.
The new depth of the object.

action.name: SaveImage
action.comment: Saves the graphics of a surface object.
action.prototype: ERR acSaveImage(*Object, OBJECTID Dest, CLASSID ClassID)

input.param: Refers to an object that will receive the encoded image data.
The Picture class to use for encoding the image data.

description.p: SaveImage() renders the visible content of a surface into an image and writes it to the destination object supplied in the action arguments. Visible child surfaces in the captured region are included in the resulting image.

The image format is selected with the ClassID argument. Supported values are Picture-compatible image classes such as CLASSID::JPEG and CLASSID::PICTURE (PNG). If ClassID is CLASSID::NIL, the default Picture implementation is used.
Errors returned while copying individual child surfaces can be propagated from CopySurface.

result.error: The intermediate picture object could not be initialised or saved.
A search routine in this function failed
Attempting to lock an object failed
The intermediate picture object could not be created.

action.name: Show
action.comment: Shows a surface object on the display.

description.p: Show() makes the surface visible. For top-level surfaces it shows the hosted display window; for child surfaces it sets the visible flag, redraws the surface and exposes the affected area. If the surface is modal, it becomes the active modal surface until it is hidden.

method.name: AddCallback
method.comment: Inserts a function hook into the drawing process of a surface object.

input.param: Callback routine to insert or move in the draw callback list.

description.p: The AddCallback() method installs a draw callback for custom rendering directly into a surface. During a redraw, callbacks are invoked in subscription order and receive the target Bitmap for the surface.
The C/C++ callback prototype is ERR Function(APTR Context, objSurface *Surface, objBitmap *Bitmap, APTR Meta). The Tiri callback prototype is function draw(Surface, Bitmap).

Callbacks may draw to the bitmap as they would to any other Bitmap. Use the Surface Width and Height fields to determine the drawable area, and respect the bitmap clipping region when writing pixels directly.
Calling AddCallback() with a callback that is already registered for the same object moves that callback to the end of the callback list, making it run after the callbacks that remain before it.

result.error: Public objects cannot draw directly to surfaces.
The callback list has reached its maximum size.
The callback list could not be expanded.

method.name: ExposeToDisplay
method.comment: Redraws a surface region to the display, preferably from its graphics buffer.
method.prototype: ERR drw::ExposeToDisplay(OBJECTPTR Object, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height, EXF Flags)
method.tiriPrototype: err = ExposeToDisplay(X:num, Y:num, Width:num, Height:num, Flags:num)

input.param: X coordinate of the expose area.
Y coordinate of the expose area.
Width of the expose area.
Height of the expose area.
Optional flags.

description.p: Call the ExposeToDisplay() method to copy a surface region to the display. The functionality is identical to that of the ExposeSurface function. Please refer to it for further documentation.

method.name: InvalidateRegion
method.comment: Redraws all of the content in a surface object.
method.prototype: ERR drw::InvalidateRegion(OBJECTPTR Object, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height)

input.param: X coordinate of the region to invalidate.
Y coordinate of the region to invalidate.
Width of the region to invalidate.
Height of the region to invalidate.

description.p: Invalidating a surface object will cause everything within a specified area to be redrawn. This includes child surface objects that intersect with the area that you have specified. Parent regions that overlap are not included in the redraw.

To quickly redraw an entire surface object's content, call this method directly without supplying an argument structure. If you want to redraw a surface object and ignore all of its surface children then you should use the Draw action instead of this method.

If you want to refresh a surface area to the display then you should use the ExposeToDisplay method instead. Exposing will use the graphics buffer to refresh the graphics, thus avoiding the speed loss of a complete redraw.

result.error: Failed to access the internal surface list.

method.name: Minimise
method.comment: For hosted surfaces only, this method will minimise the surface to an icon.

description.p: If a surface is hosted in a desktop window, calling Minimise() performs the host platform's default minimise action on that window. On Microsoft Windows, this normally minimises the window to the taskbar.

Calling Minimise() on a surface that is already in the minimised state may result in the host window being restored to the desktop. This behaviour is platform dependent and should be manually tested to confirm its reliability on the host platform.

method.name: RemoveCallback
method.comment: Removes a callback previously inserted by AddCallback().

input.param: Callback routine to remove, or NULL to remove all associated callback routines for the caller.

description.p: RemoveCallback() removes a draw callback that was previously inserted by AddCallback.
This method is scope restricted. A caller can remove only callbacks associated with its own object context, so callbacks added by other objects are not affected.

result.error: The requested callback was not found.

method.name: ResetDimensions
method.comment: Changes the dimensions of a surface.
method.prototype: ERR drw::ResetDimensions(OBJECTPTR Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE XOffset, DOUBLE YOffset, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height, DMF Dimensions)
method.tiriPrototype: err = ResetDimensions(X:num, Y:num, XOffset:num, YOffset:num, Width:num, Height:num, Dimensions:num)

input.param: New X coordinate.
New Y coordinate.
New X offset.
New Y offset.
New width.
New height.
Dimension flags.

description.p: ResetDimensions() replaces the dimensional declaration used by a surface. Use this when the relationship between fields changes, for example when a width previously derived from X and XOffset must become a fixed width.

It is not necessary to define a value for every parameter - only the ones that are relevant to the new dimension settings. For example, if X and Width are set, XOffset is ignored and the Dimensions value must include DMF::FIXED_X and DMF::FIXED_WIDTH or their scaled equivalents. Refer to the Dimensions field for the supported dimension flags.
After the new dimensions are applied, the union of the old and new areas is redrawn and exposed.

method.name: ScheduleRedraw
method.comment: Schedules a redraw operation for the next frame.

description.p: Use ScheduleRedraw() to mark a surface for redraw on the next frame cycle. The surface and its child surfaces are redrawn together, typically on the next 1/60th of a second timer tick. Direct redraw requests for the target surface are coalesced until the scheduled redraw is processed.
Scheduling is ideal in situations where a cluster of redraw events may occur within a tight time period, and it would be inefficient to draw those changes to the display individually.

Redraw schedules do not merge across the hierarchy. If both a surface and one of its children are scheduled, two redraw operations may be triggered where one would otherwise suffice. Schedule the highest relevant surface when a single redraw should cover a group of changes.

method.name: SetDisplay
method.comment: Changes the screen resolution (applies to top-level surface objects only).
method.prototype: ERR drw::SetDisplay(OBJECTPTR Object, INT X, INT Y, INT Width, INT Height, INT InsideWidth, INT InsideHeight, INT BitsPerPixel, DOUBLE RefreshRate, INT Flags)
method.tiriPrototype: err = SetDisplay(X:num, Y:num, Width:num, Height:num, InsideWidth:num, InsideHeight:num, BitsPerPixel:num, RefreshRate:num, Flags:num)

input.param: The horizontal coordinate/offset for the display.
The vertical coordinate/offset for the display.
The width of the display.
The height of the display.
The page width of the display must be the same as Width or greater.
The page height of the display must be the same as Height or greater.
Bits per pixel - 15, 16, 24 or 32.
Refresh rate.
Optional flags.

description.p: The SetDisplay method is used to change the screen resolution of the top-level surface object (which represents the screen display). It allows you to set the size of the display and you may also change the bitmap depth and the monitor's refresh rate. If successful, the change is immediate.

This method exercises some intelligence in adjusting the display to your requested settings. For instance, if the requested width and/or height is not available, the closest display setting will be chosen.
This method does not work on anything other than top-level surface objects. The current top-level surface object is usually named "SystemSurface" by default and can be searched for by that name.

result.error: The surface is not a top-level surface object.

method.name: SetOpacity
method.comment: Alters the opacity of a surface object.
method.prototype: ERR drw::SetOpacity(OBJECTPTR Object, DOUBLE Value, DOUBLE Adjustment)

input.param: New opacity multiplier, used when Adjustment is zero.
Value to add to the current opacity multiplier, or zero to assign Value directly.

description.p: SetOpacity() changes the opacity multiplier for a surface that owns its bitmap buffer. The final value is clamped by the Opacity field setter. If the surface is visible, a redraw is queued so the new opacity is reflected on the display without blocking the caller.

result.error: The surface does not own the bitmap buffer required for independent opacity.

field.name: AbsX
field.comment: The absolute horizontal position of a surface object.

description.p: This field returns the surface's horizontal position relative to the top-level surface in its local hierarchy.
Writing AbsX moves the surface so that its absolute horizontal position matches the supplied value. The surface must be initialised before this field can be changed.

field.name: AbsY
field.comment: The absolute vertical position of a surface object.

description.p: This field returns the surface's vertical position relative to the top-level surface in its local hierarchy.
Writing AbsY moves the surface so that its absolute vertical position matches the supplied value. The surface must be initialised before this field can be changed.

field.name: Align
field.comment: This field allows you to align a surface area within its owner.

description.p: Use Align to position a surface within its owner without calculating explicit coordinates. It is most useful for horizontal or vertical centring; edge alignment is normally clearer when expressed through X, Y, XOffset and YOffset.
Setting Align replaces related coordinate settings. Valid alignment flags are BOTTOM, CENTER, MIDDLE, LEFT, HORIZONTAL, RIGHT, TOP and VERTICAL.

field.name: BitsPerPixel
field.comment: Defines the pixel depth used by the surface buffer.

description.p: Set BitsPerPixel before initialisation to request a fixed pixel depth for the surface buffer. If the requested depth differs from the display or parent buffer, the graphics system converts pixels when the surface is drawn, which is usually slower than using the native display depth.
Reading this field returns the active tracked depth for the surface. A value of zero is returned if the surface is not available in the display surface list.

field.name: Bottom
field.comment: Returns the bottom-most coordinate of a surface object, Y + Height.

field.name: BottomLimit
field.comment: Prevents a surface object from moving beyond a given point at the bottom of its container.

description.p: Set BottomLimit to reserve a margin at the bottom of the parent container. For example, a value of 5 prevents Move from placing the surface inside the bottom-most five coordinate units.
Movement limits apply only to Move. Direct writes to coordinate fields bypass them.

field.name: Buffer
field.comment: Refers to the Bitmap that stores the surface's graphics.

description.p: Each surface is assigned a bitmap buffer for drawing. In many cases this buffer is shared between multiple surfaces, so clients should treat it as an implementation detail unless no public drawing API provides the required access.
The bitmap is an off-screen buffer. Direct changes to it are not exposed to the display automatically.

field.name: Colour
field.comment: Defines the background colour used when clearing the surface.

description.p: Set this field when a surface should be cleared to a solid background colour before drawing. String values may use #RRGGBB hexadecimal notation or Red,Green,Blue decimal components.
A surface that does not define a colour is not cleared during redraw. In that case, draw the full background manually to avoid stale or uninitialised graphics appearing in exposed areas.
A preset colour can be disabled by writing NULL, which clears the colour by setting its alpha component to zero. Changing Colour does not schedule a redraw.

field.name: Cursor
field.comment: Sets the pointer image used while the mouse is over the surface.

description.p: The pointer automatically switches to the selected cursor image when it enters the surface area.
The available cursor image settings are listed in the Pointer.CursorID documentation.
The Cursor field may be written with a valid cursor name or cursor ID.

field.name: Dimensions
field.comment: Indicates currently active dimension settings.

description.p: The Dimensions flags identify which coordinate and size fields currently define the surface layout, and whether those values are fixed or scaled against the parent.

This field is normally managed automatically when fields such as X, Y, Width, Height, XOffset and YOffset are changed. If manual configuration is required, avoid conflicting fixed and scaled variants on the same axis.

field.name: Display
field.comment: Refers to the Display object that manages the surface's graphics.

description.p: All surfaces belong to a Display object that manages drawing to the user's video display. This field identifies the display that owns the surface.

field.name: Drag
field.comment: Defines the Surface that moves when this surface is dragged.

description.p: Set this field to the Surface that should move when the user starts a click-drag operation over the current surface. For example, a window title bar would set Drag to the window surface. A surface may also set Drag to itself for icon-like or small draggable objects.
Set Drag to zero to disable drag handling.

field.name: DragStatus
field.comment: Reports the current drag state when dragging is enabled.

description.p: Read this field to determine whether the surface is idle, anchored for dragging or actively being moved.

field.name: Flags
field.comment: Controls optional surface behaviour.

description.p: Use Flags to enable optional surface behaviours. Preserve existing flags when updating this field, typically by combining the current value with the flags being added.
Read-only flags cannot be changed by writing this field. Initialisation-only flags can be set before the surface is initialised, but are preserved once the surface is active.

field.name: Height
field.comment: Defines the height of a surface object.

description.p: Use Height to read or change the surface height. Alternatively, call Resize to change Width and Height together.
By default the value is a fixed coordinate unit. With the FD_SCALED flag, the value is treated as a multiplier of the parent surface height.
Changing Height on a visible surface updates the displayed area immediately, including any child surfaces that need to be redrawn or resized.
Before initialisation, setting Height to zero or less clears the height dimension so that Y and YOffset can define the height dynamically. After initialisation, values of zero or less are invalid.

field.name: LeftLimit
field.comment: Prevents a surface object from moving beyond a given point on the left-hand side.

description.p: Set LeftLimit to reserve a margin at the left-hand side of the parent container. For example, a value of 3 prevents Move from placing the surface inside the left-most three coordinate units.
Movement limits apply only to Move. Direct writes to coordinate fields bypass them.

field.name: MaxHeight
field.comment: Prevents the height of a surface object from exceeding a certain value.

description.p: Set MaxHeight to limit the maximum height that can be applied through resizing. Resize cannot increase the surface beyond this value.
Direct writes to Height bypass this limit.

field.name: MaxWidth
field.comment: Prevents the width of a surface object from exceeding a certain value.

description.p: Set MaxWidth to limit the maximum width that can be applied through resizing. Resize cannot increase the surface beyond this value.
Direct writes to Width bypass this limit.

field.name: MinHeight
field.comment: Prevents the height of a surface object from shrinking beyond a certain value.

description.p: Set MinHeight to limit the minimum height that can be applied through resizing. Resize cannot shrink the surface below this value.
Direct writes to Height bypass this limit. Values less than 1 are clamped to 1.

field.name: MinWidth
field.comment: Prevents the width of a surface object from shrinking beyond a certain value.

description.p: Set MinWidth to limit the minimum width that can be applied through resizing. Resize cannot shrink the surface below this value.
Direct writes to Width bypass this limit. Values less than 1 are clamped to 1.

field.name: Modal
field.comment: Sets the surface as modal (prevents user interaction with other surfaces).

description.p: If set to true, the surface becomes the program's modal surface when shown. This prevents interaction with other surfaces until the modal surface is hidden, destroyed or no longer modal. Children of the modal surface remain interactive.
Clearing this field restores the previous modal surface if one was recorded.

field.name: Movement
field.comment: Limits the movement of a surface object to vertical or horizontal shifts.

description.p: Set Movement to limit movement performed by Move to horizontal movement, vertical movement, both axes or neither axis. By default, surfaces can move horizontally and vertically.
This field affects Move only. Direct writes to coordinate fields bypass the movement restriction.

field.name: Opacity
field.comment: Defines the translucency applied when drawing a surface.

description.p: Opacity is expressed as a normalised multiplier. The default value of 1.0 draws the surface as fully opaque. Lower values make the surface more transparent. Values outside the allowable range are clipped.

Non-opaque surfaces are drawn by rendering the surface content to its internal buffer and blending it with the background graphics. This can be costly, and the pre-copy feature may give better results for some compositions.
Translucency can significantly increase CPU usage.

field.name: Parent
field.comment: Identifies the parent Surface.

description.p: Child surfaces use this field to identify their parent. Top-level surfaces have no parent.

If Parent is not set before initialisation, the surface class searches the ownership chain for the nearest Surface. Set Parent to zero before initialisation to disable that automatic lookup. An initialised child surface may be reparented, but a top-level surface cannot be converted into a child surface after initialisation.

field.name: PopOver
field.comment: Keeps a surface in front of another surface in the Z order.

description.p: Set PopOver before initialisation to the object ID of a sibling Surface that this surface should stay in front of. For dialog windows, combine this field with Modal to keep the dialog in front and prevent interaction with other surfaces in the current program.
Set PopOver to zero before initialisation to use normal Z-order behaviour.
This field cannot be changed after initialisation. The value must identify a Surface; otherwise ERR::InvalidObject is returned.

field.name: Right
field.comment: Returns the right-most coordinate of a surface object, X + Width.

field.name: RightLimit
field.comment: Prevents a surface object from moving beyond a given point on the right-hand side.

description.p: Set RightLimit to reserve a margin at the right-hand side of the parent container. For example, a value of 8 prevents Move from placing the surface inside the right-most eight coordinate units.
Movement limits apply only to Move. Direct writes to coordinate fields bypass them.

field.name: Root
field.comment: Surface that is acting as a root for many surface children (useful when applying translucency)

field.name: TopLimit
field.comment: Prevents a surface object from moving beyond a given point at the top of its container.

description.p: Set TopLimit to reserve a margin at the top of the parent container. For example, a value of 10 prevents Move from placing the surface inside the top-most ten coordinate units.
Movement limits apply only to Move. Direct writes to coordinate fields bypass them.

field.name: UserFocus
field.comment: Refers to the surface object that has the current user focus.

description.p: Returns the object ID of the surface that has the primary user focus. Returns zero if no surface has focus.

field.name: Visible
field.comment: Indicates the visibility of a surface object.

description.p: Read this field to determine whether the surface itself is marked visible. A true value means the surface is shown; false means it is hidden.
Effective on-screen visibility also depends on the parent chain. A visible child of a hidden parent is not displayed.
Set this field, or call Hide and Show, to change the surface visibility.

field.name: VisibleHeight
field.comment: The visible height of the surface area, relative to its parents.

description.p: Read VisibleX, VisibleY, VisibleWidth and VisibleHeight to determine the portion of the surface that is visible within the parent chain.

The visible area is calculated by clipping the surface area against its parent surfaces. If a 100-unit-high surface is inside a 50-unit-high parent, at most 50 units can be visible, depending on the surface position.
If none of the surface area is visible, zero is returned. The result is never negative.

field.name: VisibleWidth
field.comment: The visible width of the surface area, relative to its parents.

description.p: Read VisibleX, VisibleY, VisibleWidth and VisibleHeight to determine the portion of the surface that is visible within the parent chain.

The visible area is calculated by clipping the surface area against its parent surfaces. If a 100-unit-wide surface is inside a 50-unit-wide parent, at most 50 units can be visible, depending on the surface position.
If none of the surface area is visible, zero is returned. The result is never negative.

field.name: VisibleX
field.comment: The first visible X coordinate of the surface area, relative to its parents.

description.p: Read VisibleX, VisibleY, VisibleWidth and VisibleHeight to determine the portion of the surface that is visible within the parent chain.
VisibleX is the first visible horizontal coordinate inside the surface's own coordinate space. It is calculated by clipping the surface area against its parent surfaces.
If none of the surface area is visible, zero is returned. The result is never negative.

field.name: VisibleY
field.comment: The first visible Y coordinate of the surface area, relative to its parents.

description.p: Read VisibleX, VisibleY, VisibleWidth and VisibleHeight to determine the portion of the surface that is visible within the parent chain.
VisibleY is the first visible vertical coordinate inside the surface's own coordinate space. It is calculated by clipping the surface area against its parent surfaces.
If none of the surface area is visible, zero is returned. The result is never negative.

field.name: Width
field.comment: Defines the width of a surface object.

description.p: Use Width to read or change the surface width. Alternatively, call Resize to change Width and Height together.
By default the value is a fixed coordinate unit. With the FD_SCALED flag, the value is treated as a multiplier of the parent surface width.
Changing Width on a visible surface updates the displayed area immediately, including any child surfaces that need to be redrawn or resized.
Before initialisation, setting Width to zero or less clears the width dimension so that X and XOffset can define the width dynamically. After initialisation, values of zero or less are invalid.

field.name: WindowHandle
field.comment: Refers to a surface object's window handle, if relevant.

description.p: This field exposes the host window handle for platforms that provide one. It is currently relevant when creating a primary surface within an X11 window manager or Microsoft Windows.
Set WindowHandle before initialisation to attach the surface to an existing native window. The field is immutable after initialisation.

field.name: WindowType
field.comment: Defines how a top-level surface is represented by a hosted desktop.

description.p: This field affects hosted desktops such as Windows and X11. It only applies to top-level surfaces that have no parent. Child surfaces ignore this field, and surfaces created inside the desktop area treat the desktop as their parent.
Custom surfaces remain responsible for their own window controls, such as title bars and resize borders.

field.name: X
field.comment: Determines the horizontal position of a surface object.

description.p: Use X to read or change the horizontal position of the surface relative to its parent.
By default the value is a fixed coordinate unit. With the FD_SCALED flag, the value is treated as a multiplier of the parent surface width.
Changing X on a visible surface updates its position immediately. If XOffset also defines the right-hand edge, the surface width is recalculated to preserve that offset.

field.name: XOffset
field.comment: Determines the horizontal offset of a surface object.

description.p: XOffset defines a distance from the right-hand edge of the parent surface.
When X is set and Width is not set, XOffset makes the surface width dynamic. The width extends from X to the parent width minus XOffset.
When Width is set, XOffset positions the surface from the parent right-hand edge: X = ParentWidth - Width - XOffset.

field.name: Y
field.comment: Determines the vertical position of a surface object.

description.p: Use Y to read or change the vertical position of the surface relative to its parent.
By default the value is a fixed coordinate unit. With the FD_SCALED flag, the value is treated as a multiplier of the parent surface height.
Changing Y on a visible surface updates its position immediately.

field.name: YOffset
field.comment: Determines the vertical offset of a surface object.

description.p: YOffset defines a distance from the bottom edge of the parent surface.
When Y is set and Height is not set, YOffset makes the surface height dynamic. The height extends from Y to the parent height minus YOffset.
When Height is set, YOffset positions the surface from the parent bottom edge: Y = ParentHeight - Height - YOffset.

constants.comment: Universal values for alignment of graphics and text
constants.const: Align to bottom
Synonym for HORIZONTAL | VERTICAL
Align to horizontal center
Align to left
Synonym for HORIZONTAL | VERTICAL
Align to right
Align to top
Align to vertical center
Enable alpha blending to the destination if the source supports an alpha channel.
Special CopyArea option that avoids blending when the destination pixel is empty.
Perform dithering if the colour formats differ between the source and destination.
For primitive operations such as DrawRectangle, this will fill the shape with a solid colour or texture.
Use linear interpolation to improve the quality of alpha blending.
Allow the use of dithering to improve image quality at a cost of speed.
Redraw the surface before performing the copy operation.

Use of the BUFFER option allows the cursor request to be buffered in the event that the cursor is locked at the time of calling the SetCursor function. Use of this flag is highly recommended in most circumstances, but may not be used in conjunction with the ANCHOR option.
Release the cursor after the left mouse button is held and released.
Release the cursor after the middle mouse button is held and released.
Set the cursor only on the condition that the user is not holding down any buttons. ERR::NothingDone is returned if the user has a button held down.
Similar to the anchor option, but allows the pointer to move within the surface referred to by ObjectID.
Release the cursor after the right mouse button is held and released.
Enable alpha blending if the source is in 32-bit colour format with an alpha channel.
Enable clipping of the source coordinates.
Ignore the colour format defined in the source surface (if any) and generate a default format based on the BitsPerPixel value.
Adjust X and Y coordinates by the offset values defined in the Surface.XOffset and Surface.YOffset fields.
Perform a translucent copy operation, using the strength value specified in the Surface.Opacity field.
Enable transparent copying, whereby colours matching the source's Colour field will be ignored.
The CenterX field is a fixed size.
The CenterY field is a fixed size.
The Depth field is a fixed size.
The Height field is a fixed size.
The RadiusX field is a fixed size.
The RadiusY field is a fixed size.
The Width field is a fixed suze.
The X field is a fixed coordinate.
The XOffset field is a fixed coordinate.
The Y field is a fixed coordinate.
The YOffset field is a fixed coordinate.
The Z field is a fixed coordinate.
The CenterX field is scaled to this object's parent.
The CenterY field is scaled to this object's parent.
The Depth field is scaled to this object's parent.
The Height field is scaled to this object's parent.
The RadiusX field is scaled to this object's parent.
The RadiusY field is a scaled size to this object's parent.
The Width field is scaled to this object's parent.
The X field is scaled to this object's parent.
The XOffset field is scaled to this object's parent.
The Y field is scaled to this object's parent.
The YOffset field is scaled to this object's parent.
The Z field is a scaled coordinate to this object's parent.
The surface is being dragged and the mouse pointer is anchored to the surface.
The surface is not being dragged.
The surface is being dragged.
The display is driven by OpenGLES.
The display is native (supported by internal drivers).
The display is driven by Microsoft Windows drivers.
The display is driven by the X Window System (X11, X.Org, XFree86)

constants.comment: Optional flags for the ExposeSurface() function.
constants.const: The supplied coordinates for exposure are absolute (relative to the display).
The supplied coordinates for exposure are absolute (relative to the display).
If set, all child surfaces that intersect with exposed region will be included in the expose operation.
Redraw every volatile object that intersects with the expose region, including internal volatile children.

Only redraw volatile objects that obscure the expose region from a position outside of the target surface and its children. Useful if no redrawing has occurred in the surface, but the surface has moved to a new position and the parents need to be redrawn.
The hosted display sticks to the front.
The hosted display is given a taskbar button.
Change the alpha channel level for the entire window.
Defines an RGB colour that is to be used as transparent.
All new displays are represented in the system tray when this option is active.

constants.comment: JET constants are documented in GetInputEvent()
constants.const: The X, Y values are defined as absolute coordinates, relative to the top-left of the display.
Left mouse button; XBox A button, PS square button. Value is pressure sensitive, ranging between 0 - 1.0 (0 is released, 1.0 is fully depressed).
Non-specific button assignment.
Right mouse button; XBox X button, PS cross button.
Middle mouse button; XBox Y button, PS triangle.
Alt. mouse button 1; XBox B button, PS circle.
Alt. mouse button 2.
Non-specific button assignment.
Non-specific button assignment.
Non-specific button assignment.
Non-specific button assignment.
This message is sent by the input system when the mouse pointer enters an area for the first time. The message value refers to the object ID of the container being monitored for movement.
This message is sent by the input system when the mouse pointer leaves an area. The message value refers to the object ID of the container being monitored for movement.
Controller tilted on the X/Y axis. Value indicates angle, -ve = left, +ve = right
Controller is rising or falling. Value expressed as 'speed',
Recently supplied input occurred at the edge of the display.
Pen tilt angle. 0 is pointing down directly with nib at bottom, 0.5 is 90 degrees, 1.0 is inversed (eraser at bottom)
Amount of pressure applied, ranges from 0 (none) to 1.0 (normal) and possibly higher if user presses hard enough
Mouse wheel rotation - the value generally reflects the number of 'clicks' rotated on the wheel.
Some mouse wheels can be tilted to the left or right. Ranges from -1.0 to +1.0

constants.comment: JTYPE flags are used to categorise input types.
constants.const: Analog movement (ranging from -1.0 to 1.0)
Cursor has been anchored with LockCursor()
Input is a physical button or switch
Crossing events manage the entering and leaving of an area.
Set by the input system if the Type is a button and the button has been clicked in quick succession so as to be classed as a double-click.
D-Pad or digital joystick source (restricted to +/- 1)
Set if sufficient movement occurred between the original click and its point of release (usually requires a 3 or more pixel difference).
This special flag is set by the input system if the pointer is click-dragging an object at the time of the event.
Extended or indirect movement information. This covers all types of movement that are unconnected to coordinate positioning - mouse wheel movement and pen tilt are two such examples.
X/Y coordinate movement only. Movement such as the wheel mouse spinning is not covered by this type as it does not influence the coordinate system.
Input is a repeated entry (i.e. user is holding down a button and a repetition timer is being triggered)
Indicates to the receiver of this message that it is not the primary/original recipient
The cross hair is used for targeting specific pixel points (common in paint programs).
Works in conjunction with the SetCustomCursor() function to represent a program defined bitmap.
The default cursor (usually an arrow pointing to the upper left).
Used to indicate that a surface or object can be dragged by the user.
The hand cursor is often used for indicating click-able content (hyper-links, icons etc).
Similar to the standard hand cursor, but points to the left.
Similar to the standard hand cursor, but points to the right.
The cursor graphic is invisible (but will continue to operate as normal in all other respects).
Represents a magnifying glass.
The paintbrush cursor is typically employed by paint programs.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the bottom edge of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the bottom left corner of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the bottom right corner of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the left edge of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the right edge of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the top edge of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the top left corner of any rectangular area.
Sizing cursor - for resizing the top right corner of any rectangular area.
Multi-directional sizing cursor - for resizing in any direction.
The sleep cursor is used to inform the user that the computer is busy.
The horizontal split cursor is typically used for splitting rectangles in half, or dragging a horizontal split within a large rectangular space.
The vertical split cursor is typically used for splitting rectangles in half, or dragging a vertical split within a large rectangular space.
The stop cursor is used to inform the user that an operation is not possible (e.g. drag and drop to an unsupported object area).
The text cursor is popular for the precise positioning of text cursors.

constants.comment: Switches for the Surface class' Flags field.
constants.const: Read-only. Indicates that after-copy mode has been enabled.
When resizing, enforce the aspect ratio as defined by the diagonal from Surface.MinWidth, Surface.MinHeight to Surface.MaxWidth, Surface.MaxHeight.

The surface object will send a quit message to its supporting process when and if the Close method is called. This flag is typically used when a surface object represents a core window for an application.
Do not copy background information into the surface buffer - composite on the fly instead
This flag is set if the Disable action has been called on a surface object. Calling the Enable action will turn off the flag setting.
Passes the NEVER_SHRINK option to the surface bitmap
The target buffer always remains at the same depth
Allow the surface to open as a new screen display
Helps application windows manage the user's focus within the window
Read-only. If set, this flag indicates that the surface object currently has the focus.
Define host on initialisation to create a container that can host surfaces from other processes.
Focus is diverted directly to the parent
Synonym for HOST | TRANSPARENT | DISABLED | PRECOPY | VIDEO | FIXED_BUFFER | PERVASIVE_COPY | FIXED_DEPTH | FULL_SCREEN | IGNORE_FOCUS
Prevents any kind of focussing on this object; no circumvention is possible
Turns off all horizontal movement (applies to the Move() action only).
Do not copy background information into the surface buffer - composite on the fly instead
Turns off all vertical movement (applies to the Move() action only).
This flag can be set in conjunction with after-copy mode. It forces the after-copy support routine to copy graphics over the entire surface area, rather than avoiding the graphics of child surfaces.
Do not copy background information into the surface buffer - composite on the fly instead
Enables pre-copy mode, which means that all graphics behind the surface object are copied into the bitmap buffer prior to any redraw. This mode can have a noticable impact on CPU time when drawing.
Synonym for HAS_FOCUS | CURSOR | AFTER_COPY
Prevents any response to the Move action. It can be circumvented by writing to coordinate fields directly.
Enable if the surface object must stick to the back of its container.
Enable if the surface object must stick to the front of its container.
Perform a total redraw of the entire surface when drawing - no partial draws
Enables transparency, which means that the internal graphics routines will ignore this surface during redraws. It is typically used when creating containers that will host other surfaces.

Set this flag if you would like the surface object's data to be managed in video memory only. While this can give some speed advantages, be warned that video based surfaces are limited to write-only operations.
If a surface object is visible to the user, the VISIBLE flag will be set. If the flag is not set, the surface object is hidden.
Synonym for PRECOPY | AFTER_COPY | CURSOR

Set this flag if you would like the surface object's data to be managed in video memory only. While this can give some speed advantages, be warned that video based surfaces are limited to write-only operations.
Can be used by window surfaces to identify themselves as a root layer.

constants.comment: Options for the Surface WindowType field.
constants.const: Default to the standard hosted window mode with full titlebar, borders and taskbar representation.
Create a borderless (custom) window with icon tray representation.
Create a borderless (custom) window with no UI representation.
Create a borderless (custom) window with taskbar representation.

struct.name: BitmapSurface
struct.field: Pointer to the bitmap graphics data.
The distance between bitmap lines, measured in bytes.
The number of bits per pixel (8, 15, 16, 24, 32).
The number of bytes per pixel (1, 2, 3, 4).
Opacity level of the source if CSRF::TRANSLUCENT is used.
Colour index to use if CSRF::TRANSPARENT is used.
A clipping rectangle will restrict drawing operations to this region if CSRF::CLIP is used.
Offset all X coordinate references by the given value.
Offset all Y coordinate references by the given value.
The colour format of this bitmap's pixels, or alternatively use CSRF::DEFAULT_FORMAT.

struct.name: ColourFormat
struct.field: Right shift value for red (15/16 bit formats only)
Unshifted mask value for red (ranges from 0x00 to 0xff)
Number of bits per pixel for this format.

struct.name: CursorInfo
struct.field: Maximum cursor width for custom cursors
Maximum cursor height for custom cursors
Preferred bits-per-pixel setting for custom cursors

struct.name: DisplayInfo
struct.field: Object ID related to the display
The colour format to use for each pixel.

struct.name: InputEvent
struct.field: The value associated with the Type
Surface that the input message is being conveyed to
Surface that is directly under the mouse pointer at the time of the event
Absolute horizontal position of mouse cursor (relative to the top left of the display)
Absolute vertical position of mouse cursor (relative to the top left of the display)
Horizontal position relative to the surface that the pointer is over - unless a mouse button is held or pointer is anchored - then the coordinates are relative to the click-held surface
Vertical position relative to the surface that the pointer is over - unless a mouse button is held or pointer is anchored - then the coordinates are relative to the click-held surface
The hardware device that this event originated from
JET constant that describes the event
Broad descriptors for the given Type (see JTYPE flags). Automatically defined when delivered to the pointer object
Mask to use for checking against subscribers

struct.name: SurfaceInfo
struct.field: Object that contains the surface area
Refers to the display if this object is at root level
Bits per pixel of the bitmap
Bytes per pixel of the bitmap
Line width of the bitmap, in bytes
